scope.summary:
Implement WYSIWYG rendering of the MT-011 block document model onto the egui canvas, using cosmic-text for glyph layout/shaping and winit IME events for multi-language text input. This MT delivers the core visible editing surface: a scrollable egui widget that renders the document tree as a sequence of styled paragraphs/blocks, positions a blinking text caret, accepts keyboard and IME composition input, and issues Transactions back to the MT-011 model.

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: create src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/ containing: mod.rs, line_layout.rs (cosmic-text Buffer/FontSystem management; shapes each TextLeaf's rope content with mark-derived font attributes into cosmic_text::LayoutLine items), block_renderer.rs (egui Painter calls per BlockNode kind: paragraph renders shaped lines, heading renders with scale factor, blockquote adds a left bar + tint, code_block renders monospace in a rounded rect, list items add bullets/numbers, table renders a grid), caret.rs (DocCaret: tracks Selection from MT-011, blinks at 500 ms via egui's ctx.request_repaint_after, paints a 2 px egui Painter rect at the correct pixel position resolved from cosmic-text glyph positions), ime_handler.rs (converts winit::event::Ime::Commit and Ime::Preedit into InsertText/DeleteText Transactions; preedit shown as underlined inline text), input_handler.rs (converts egui keyboard events: printable chars -> InsertText, Backspace -> DeleteText, Delete -> DeleteText forward, ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down -> selection moves, Home/End -> line/doc boundary moves, Shift+arrow -> extend selection, Ctrl+A -> select all), and rich_editor_widget.rs (the top-level egui::Widget impl: owns FontSystem + DocModel + UndoManager; update() calls block_renderer, caret, input_handler in sequence; scrolling via egui ScrollArea).

FILES TO CREATE:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/mod.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/line_layout.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/block_renderer.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/caret.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/ime_handler.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/input_handler.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/mod.rs (add pub mod renderer)

CRATES TO ADD:
- cosmic-text = '0.12' (font shaping + layout; exact version to match WP-011 spike choice or latest stable)
- winit = { version='0.30', features=['rwh_06'] } (IME events; version must match the WP-011 shell's winit version)

STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
1. In line_layout.rs, create FontSystemHolder (cosmic_text::FontSystem wrapped in a RefCell for single-threaded egui access). For each TextLeaf in a paragraph, create a cosmic_text::Attrs based on the leaf's marks (bold -> Weight::BOLD, italic -> Style::Italic, code -> family Monospace) and call Buffer::set_text. Store the resulting LayoutLine items with their pixel widths for hit-testing.
2. In block_renderer.rs, iterate the document tree. For each BlockNode: call line_layout to get shaped lines; use egui Painter::galley_with_override_text_style or raw painter.text() with the cosmic-text pixel positions. Heading: multiply font size by [1.8, 1.5, 1.25] for h1/h2/h3. Blockquote: paint a 3 px left bar in accent color, indent 16 px. code_block: use egui::Frame with rounded corners and the code background color; monospace font. list items: paint '•' or '1.' prefix. table: compute column widths, paint cell borders with painter.rect_stroke.
3. In caret.rs, after block_renderer lays out all lines, resolve the cursor DocPosition to a pixel (x, y) by summing line heights to the cursor paragraph, then scanning glyph advances within the line. Paint a 2 px wide rect of the theme's text color. Blink: toggle a bool on egui timer (ctx.input(|i| i.time) modulo 1.0 > 0.5). Call ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(250)) to drive blinks.
4. In ime_handler.rs, on winit::event::Ime::Preedit(text, cursor_range): replace the preedit region with an underlined InlineText span (use a dedicated PreeditMark); on Ime::Commit(text): finalize with InsertText transaction. Integrate with the egui-winit bridge's event loop. winit IME must be enabled via window.set_ime_allowed(true) in the shell init (document this as a shell contract requirement).
5. In input_handler.rs, handle egui Key events (egui::Key::Backspace, Delete, ArrowLeft, etc.) and egui::Event::Text for printable characters. Map to Transactions and feed to UndoManager. Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z -> undo/redo via UndoManager. All key handling must respect egui's consume_key pattern to avoid double-handling.
6. In rich_editor_widget.rs, impl egui::Widget for RichEditorWidget { fn ui(self, ui: &mut egui::Ui) -> egui::Response }. The widget owns a RichEditorState (DocModel + UndoManager + FontSystemHolder + scroll offset). ScrollArea::vertical wraps the block_renderer output.
7. AccessKit: REUSE the existing WP-011 accessibility layer (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/mod.rs + registry.rs + live.rs + snapshot.rs) to assign a stable AccessKit NodeId (author_id = 'rich-editor-root') with Role::TextInput to the top-level widget. Each paragraph block gets author_id = format!('re-block-{}', path_hash) with Role::Paragraph. The caret position is reported as AccessKit::TextPosition so screen readers and swarm agents can locate it. Register nodes through the accessibility/registry.rs API rather than calling egui::Context::set_accesskit_node directly.

DEPENDENCIES: MT-011 (BlockDocumentModel must be complete and compiling).
acceptance_criteria: A paragraph containing 'Hello **world**' (bold mark on 'world') renders visibly in the egui window: 'Hello ' in regular weight and 'world' in bold weight, confirmed by kittest screenshot assertion or visual inspection.
Typing ASCII characters into the focused editor widget inserts them at the caret position and the paragraph redraws with the new text.
Backspace at position > 0 deletes the character to the left of the caret.
ArrowLeft/ArrowRight move the caret one character; the caret repaint position changes.
Ctrl+Z undoes the last typed character (text reverts); Ctrl+Shift+Z redoes it.
IME preedit text is displayed with an underline decoration; Ime::Commit replaces the preedit with the committed text.
Heading level 1 text renders visibly larger than paragraph text (pixel height ratio >= 1.5x), confirmed by kittest or screenshot.
Code block content renders in a monospace font inside a visually distinct rounded background rect.
Blockquote renders with a visible left bar distinct from the paragraph background.
AccessKit NodeId 'rich-editor-root' with Role::TextInput is present in the accessibility tree during an egui kittest run.
cargo test for renderer module passes with 0 failures.
No foreground window pop-ups, no focus steal (HBR-QUIET compliance): the editor widget must not call request_user_attention or any OS-level focus grab.
proof_targets: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native -- renderer` (or equivalent): output shows 'test result: ok'.

Run an egui kittest screenshot test that renders a doc with a paragraph + bold span + h1 heading and saves a PNG to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt012_wysiwyg.png; attach this PNG in the validator handoff.
Run `cargo test -p handshake-native -- renderer::caret` to confirm caret blink state toggles.
Demonstrate in kittest that AccessKit tree contains a node with author_id 'rich-editor-root' and Role::TextInput.
implementation_notes: cosmic-text FontSystem must be created once and reused across frames (wrap in egui UserData or a long-lived state struct); recreating it each frame causes visible font flicker.
cosmic-text uses pixel coordinates; egui uses logical points. Convert: pixel = point * egui_ctx.pixels_per_point(). Apply this conversion before all painter calls.

winit IME integration requires the egui-winit EventLoop to forward Ime events to the widget; check that the WP-011 shell's event loop (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/main.rs + app.rs, delivered by WP-011) forwards WindowEvent::Ime variants. This is a cross-WP dependency on WP-011's shell wiring, not a same-WP MT dependency.
The caret blink timer must use egui's time (ctx.input(|i| i.time)), not std::time::Instant, to avoid fighting egui's repaint scheduling.
For the vertical-slice proof, implement paragraph + bold/italic + heading + caret first; add table/list/blockquote/code_block in subsequent passes within this MT.

AccessKit NodeIds must use AccessKit's NodeId::new(u64) seeded from a deterministic hash of the block path, not a random ID, so swarm agents can reference them by stable value across repaints; register and resolve these through the existing src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs API.
egui_kittest: use egui_kittest::Harness::new_ui(|ui| { widget.ui(ui); }) for all automated rendering tests; do not rely on manual visual inspection as the only proof.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx (overall editor widget composition: toolbar + content area + find panel)
app/src/components/TiptapEditor.tsx (EditorContent renders Tiptap's ProseMirror view; this MT is the native equivalent)
app/src/lib/editor/visual_debug.ts (EditorDebugSnapshot shape: provides model for what the accessibility/debug layer must expose)
binds_backend_api: No direct backend API calls in this MT. The renderer reads from the MT-011 DocModel which is loaded/saved by MT-020.

risks: cosmic-text pixel/point coordinate mismatch causes all glyphs to render at wrong positions on high-DPI displays.
IME preedit handling double-inserts: both the preedit characters and the commit are applied as separate transactions, corrupting the rope.
Caret blink calls request_repaint() every 250 ms even when the editor is not focused, causing 100% CPU on idle.
AccessKit NodeIds computed from a hash collide for documents with many paragraphs, causing swarm agents to target the wrong block.
Font shaping crashes or panics on empty TextLeaf (rope length 0) because cosmic-text Buffer::set_text with an empty string triggers an assertion.
Table cell rendering overflows its column bounds when cells contain long text, corrupting adjacent cell paint.
minimum_controls: All pixel/point conversions must go through a single helper fn pts_to_px(pts: f32, ctx: &egui::Context) -> f32; a unit test asserts the conversion at pixels_per_point = 2.0.
IME handler must clear the preedit region before applying Commit; a cargo test simulates Preedit then Commit and asserts rope content equals only the committed text.
Caret blink request_repaint_after must be guarded by an is_focused check on the egui response; test that an unfocused editor does not call request_repaint.
AccessKit NodeId hash function must be tested with a 500-paragraph doc to assert zero collisions.
cosmic-text Buffer::set_text must receive at least a single space for empty TextLeaf nodes; block_renderer must special-case empty ropes.
Table renderer must clip painter output to the cell's Rect using painter.with_clip_rect before painting cell content.
